title: alsocydia erubescens
intro: Alsocydia erubescens, also known as the Blush Sawfly or Blush Rose Sawfly, is a type of sawfly that is commonly found in Europe and Asia. The Blush Sawfly is not typically considered a medicinal plant, but rather an insect that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The larvae of the Blush Sawfly have been used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to their high content of analgesic and antispasmodic properties. However, I couldn't find any information on specific herbal preparations of the Blush Sawfly.
